title: Bridge Paper: Spoken language processing activates the primary visual cortex.

Paper: Spoken language processing activates the primary visual cortex.
DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0289671
Abstract: Primary visual cortex (V1) is generally thought of as a low-level sensory area that primarily processes basic visual features. Although there is evidence for multisensory effects on its activity, these are typically found for the processing of simple sounds and their properties, for example spatially or temporally-congruent simple sounds.
